bulbus allii cepae.dose: Unless otherwise prescribed: a daily dosage is 50g of fresh onion or 20g of the dried drug; doses of preparations should be calculated accordingly (14).
bulbus allii cepae.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data The principal use of Bulbus Allii Cepae today is to prevent age-dependent changes in the blood vessels, and loss of appetite (19).
bulbus allii cepae.formulations: Fresh juice and 5% and 50% ethanol extracts have been used in clinical studies (1). A “soft” extract is marketed in France but is not recognized as a drug by French authorities (7). Dried Bulbus Allii Cepae products should be stored in well-closed containers, protected from light, moisture, and elevated tempera- ture. Fresh bulbs and juice should be refrigerated (2-10°C).
bulbus allii cepae.precautions: Allergies to the plant. The level of safety of Bulbus Allii Cepae is reflected by its worldwide use as a vegetable.
bulbus allii cepae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

bulbus allii sativi.dose: Unless otherwise prescribed, average daily dose is as follows (7): fresh garlic, 2-5g; dried powder, 0.4-1.2g; oil, 2-5mg; extract, 300-1000mg (as solid material). Other preparations should correspond to 4-12mg of alliin or about 2-5mg of allicin).
bulbus allii sativi.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data As an adjuvant to dietetic management in the treatment of hyperlipidaemia, and in the prevention of atherosclerotic (age-dependent) vascular changes (5, 27-31). The drug may be useful in the treatment of mild hypertension (11, 28).
bulbus allii sativi.formulations: Fresh bulbs, dried powder, volatile oil, oil macerates, juice, aqueous or alcoholic extracts, aged garlic extracts (minced garlic that is incubated in aqueous alcohol (15-20%) for 20 months, then concentrated), and odourless garlic products (garlic products in which the alliinase has been inactivated by cooking; or in which chlorophyll has been added as a deodorant; or aged garlic preparations that have low concentrations of water-soluble sulfur compounds) (18, 24). The juice is the most unstable dosage form. Alliin and allicin decompose rapidly, and those products must be used promptly (18). Dried Bulbus Allii Sativi products should be stored in well-closed containers, protected from light, moisture, and elevated temperature.
bulbus allii sativi.precautions: Bulbus Allii Sativi is contraindicated in patients with a known allergy to the drug. The level of safety for Bulbus Allii Sativi is reflected by its worldwide use as a seasoning in food.
bulbus allii sativi.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

aloe vera.dose: The correct individual dose is the smallest amount required to produce a soft-formed stool (26). As a laxative for adults and children over 10 years old, 0.04-0.11g (Curacao or Barbados Aloe) or 0.06-0.17g (Cape Aloe) of the dried juice (6, 14), corresponding to 10-30mg hydroxyanthraquinones per day, or 0.1g as a single dose in the evening.
aloe vera.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Short-term treatment of occasional constipation (2, 12, 13, 15).
aloe vera.formulations: Powdered, dried juice and preparations thereof for oral use.
aloe vera.precautions: As with other stimulant laxatives, products containing Aloe should not be used in patients with intestinal obstruction or stenosis, atony, severe dehydration with electrolyte depletion, or chronic constipation (28). Aloe should not be administered to patients with inflammatory intestinal diseases, such as appen- dicitis, Crohn disease, ulcerative colitis, irritable bowel syndrome, or diverticuliti
aloe vera.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

bupleurum falcatum.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
bupleurum falcatum.formulations: Decoction (5). Store crude plant material in a dry environment protected from moths, light, and moisture (1, 2).
bupleurum falcatum.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

herba centellae.dose: Oral dose: 0.33-0.68g or by oral infusion of a similar amount three times daily (4-6).
herba centellae.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Treatment of wounds, burns, and ulcerous skin ailments, and prevention of keloid and hypertrophic scars (10, 18-21). Extracts of the plant have been employed to treat second- and third-degree burns (19). Extracts have been used topically to accelerate healing, particularly in cases of chronic postsurgical and post-trauma wounds (19). Extracts have been administered orally to treat stress- induced stomach and duodenal ulcers (10).
herba centellae.formulations: Dried drug for infusion (18); galenic preparations for oral administration (10). Powder or extract (liquid or ointment) for topical application (1, 4). Package in well-closed, light-resistant containers.
herba centellae.precautions: Allergy to plants of the Apiaceae family.
herba centellae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

flos chamomillae.dose: Internal use Adult dose of flower head: average daily dose 2-8g, 3 times a day (7, 8, 11); of fluid extract 1:1 in 45% ethanol: dose 1-4ml, 3 times a day (6, 11). Child dose of flower head: 2g, 3 times daily; of fluid extract (ethanol 45-60%): single dose 0.6-2ml (11). Should not be used by children under 3 years old.
flos chamomillae.formulations: Dried flower-heads, liquid extract (1:1 in 45% alcohol), tinctures and other galenicals (11). Store in well-closed containers, protected from light (1-3).
flos chamomillae.precautions: Camomile is contraindicated in patients with a known sensitivity or allergy to plants of the Asteraceae (Compositae) such as ragweed, asters, and chrysanthe- mums (21).
flos chamomillae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

the shoots grown.dose: Crude drug-average daily dose, 2-4g (7); volatile oil-average daily dose, 0.05-0.2g (7); other preparations-average daily dose as above (7).
the shoots grown.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
the shoots grown.formulations: Crude plant material, powder, volatile oil, other galenic preparations. Store in a well-closed glass or metal container (do not use plastic), protected from light and moisture (1-6, 10).
the shoots grown.precautions: The drug is contraindicated in cases of fever of unknown origin, pregnancy, stomach or duodenal ulcers (7, 9, 12), and in patients with an allergy to cinna- mon or Peru balsam (9).
the shoots grown.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

rhizoma coptidis.dose: Maximum daily oral dosage of crude plant material: 1.5-6g (1, 3).
rhizoma coptidis.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
rhizoma coptidis.formulations: Crude plant material, decoction, and powder. Store in a well-ventilated dry environment protected from light (1).
rhizoma coptidis.precautions: The safety of berberine or extracts of Rhizoma Coptidis in pregnancy has not been established (14). Therefore, until such data are available the use of ber- berine during pregnancy is contraindicated.
rhizoma coptidis.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

rhizoma curcumae longae.dose: Crude plant material, 3-9g daily (5, 6); powdered plant material, 1.5-3.0g daily (9, 19); oral infusion, 0.5-1g three times per day; tincture (1:10) 0.5-1ml three times per day.
rhizoma curcumae longae.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data The principal use of Rhizoma Curcumae Longae is for the treatment of acid, flatulent, or atonic dyspepsia (26-28).
rhizoma curcumae longae.formulations: Powdered crude plant material, rhizomes (1, 2), and corresponding preparations (25). Store in a dry environment protected from light. Air dry the crude drug every 2-3 months (1). α-turmerone C H3 C H3 CH3 O CH3 β-turmerone C H 3 CH3 O C H 2 CH3 ar-turmerone C H3 CH 3 O H CH 3 C H3 C H3 CH3 H CH 3 C H 3 H
rhizoma curcumae longae.precautions: Obstruction of the biliary tract. In cases of gallstones, use only after consulta- tion with a physician (26). Hypersensitivity to the drug.
rhizoma curcumae longae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

echinacea angustifolia.dose: E. angustifolia root Unless otherwise prescribed, hot water (about 150ml) is poured over about 0.5 teaspoon (about 1g) of powdered plant material, allowed to steep for 10 min- utes, passed through a strainer, and taken orally three times a day between meals (7). Liquid extract (1:5, 45% ethanol), 0.5-1ml three times daily (7). Tincture (1:5, 45% ethanol), 2-5ml three times daily (7). E. pallida root Unless otherwise prescribed: daily dose, tincture (1:5 with 50% ethanol by volume) from original dry extract (50% ethanol), corresponding to 900mg of root (9).
echinacea angustifolia.formulations: Powdered roots, and galenics and preparations thereof for internal use (9). are considered to be non-toxic (7, 20), and since they lack the 1,2-unsaturated
echinacea angustifolia.precautions: External use Allergy to plants in the Asteraceae.
echinacea angustifolia.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

herba echinaceae purpureae.dose: Oral daily dosage of Herba Echinaceae Purpureae, 6-9ml expressed juice (1) for no longer than 8 successive weeks (1). External use of semisolid preparations containing at least 15% pressed juice (1) for no longer than 8 successive weeks (1). Information on dosages for children is not available (7).
herba echinaceae purpureae.formulations: Powdered aerial part, pressed juice and galenic preparations thereof for internal and external use (1, 3).
herba echinaceae purpureae.precautions: External use Allergy to the plant.
herba echinaceae purpureae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

herba ephedrae.dose: Crude plant material: 1-6g for decoction daily (8, 21). Liquid extract (1:1 in 45% alcohol): 1-3ml daily (21). Tincture (1:4 in 45% alcohol): 6-8ml daily (21).
herba ephedrae.formulations: Powdered plant material; extracts and other galenicals. Store in well closed, light-resistant containers.
herba ephedrae.precautions: Herba Ephedrae should not be administered to patients with coronary thrombosis, diabetes, glaucoma, heart disease, hypertension, thyroid disease, impaired circulation of the cerebrum, phaeochromocytoma, or enlarged pros- tate (10, 21, 23). Co-administration of Herba Ephedrae preparations with
herba ephedrae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

folium ginkgo.dose: Dried extract (as described in Dosage forms), 120-240mg daily in 2 or 3 divided doses (2); 40mg extract is equivalent to 1.4-2.7g leaves (20). Fluid extract (1:1), 0.5ml 3 times a day (1, 2).
folium ginkgo.formulations: Standardized extracts (dry extracts from dried leaves, extracted with acetone and water, drug:extract ratio 35-67:1) contain 22-27% flavone glycosides and 5-7% terpene lactones, of which approximately 2.8-3.4% consists of ginkgolides A, B, and C and 2.6-3.2% bilobalide. The level of ginkgolic acids is below 5mg/kg. Coated tablets and solution for oral administration are prepared from standardized purified extracts (20, 21).
folium ginkgo.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

panax ginseng.dose: Unless otherwise prescribed, daily dose (taken in the morning): dried root 0.5-2g by decoction; doses of other preparations should be calculated accord- ingly (21, 23, 89).
panax ginseng.formulations: Crude plant material, capsules and tablets of powdered drugs, extracts, tonic drinks, wines, and lozenges. Store in a cool, dry place in well-sealed containers (20).
panax ginseng.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

glycyrrhiza glabra.dose: Unless otherwise prescribed, average daily dose of crude plant material, 5-15g, corresponding to 200-800mg of glycyrrhizin. Doses of other preparations should be calculated accordingly (29). Radix Glycyrrhizae should not be used for longer than 4-6 weeks without medical advice.
glycyrrhiza glabra.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
glycyrrhiza glabra.formulations: Crude plant material, dried extract and liquid extract. Store in a well-closed container, protected from light and moisture (1, 3).
glycyrrhiza glabra.precautions: Radix Glycyrrhizae is contraindicated in patients with hypertension, cholestatic disorders or cirrhosis of the liver, hypokalaemia, or chronic renal insufficiency, and during pregnancy (9, 29).
glycyrrhiza glabra.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

paeonia lactiflora pallas.dose: Maximum daily oral dose of crude plant material, 6-15g (2), standardized for paeoniflorin.
paeonia lactiflora pallas.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
paeonia lactiflora pallas.formulations: Crude plant material, powder, and decoction. Store in a ventilated dry environ- ment protected from light (2). O O O O OH OH O H CH2OH O O O H CH3 H H
paeonia lactiflora pallas.precautions: Reports of traditional use indicate that Radix Paeoniae may have abortifacient activity; therefore, the use of Radix Paeoniae in pregnancy is contraindicated (32).
paeonia lactiflora pallas.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

plantago afra.dose: The suggested average dose is 7.5g dissolved in 240ml water or juice taken orally 1-3 times daily depending on the individual response. The recommended dose for children aged 6-12 years is one-half the adult dose. For children under 6 years, a physician should be consulted. An additional glass of liquid is
plantago afra.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data As a bulk-forming laxative used to restore and maintain regularity (2, 4, 16-20). Semen Plantaginis is indicated in the treatment of chronic constipation, tempo- rary constipation due to illness or pregnancy, irritable bowel syndrome, consti- pation related to duodenal ulcer or diverticulitis (17-22). It is also used to soften the stools of those with haemorrhoids, or after anorectal surgery (16, 17).
plantago afra.formulations: Seeds, powder, and granules. Store in well-closed containers, in a cool dry place, protected from light (1-4).
plantago afra.precautions: Known hypersensitivity or allergy to the plant; faecal impaction or intestinal obstruction; diabetes mellitus where insulin adjustment is difficult (27).
plantago afra.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

platycodon grandiflorum.dose: The usual dose range is 2-9g daily (1, 3, 6).
platycodon grandiflorum.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
platycodon grandiflorum.formulations: Dried roots, extracts, and other preparations.
platycodon grandiflorum.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

rauvolfia serpentina.dose: Powder, 200mg daily in divided doses for 1-3 weeks; maintenance 50-300mg daily (1). Doses of other preparations should be calculated accordingly. Doses of
rauvolfia serpentina.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data The principal use today is in the treatment of mild essential hypertension (16- 22). Treatment is usually administered in combination with a diuretic agent to support the drug’s antihypertensive activity, and to prevent fluid retention which may develop if Radix Rauwolfiae is given alone (18).
rauvolfia serpentina.formulations: Crude drug and powder. Package in well-closed containers and store at 15- 25°C (9) in a dry place, secure against insect attack (1).
rauvolfia serpentina.precautions: Radix Rauwolfiae products are contraindicated in patients who have previously demonstrated hypersensitivity to the plant or its alkaloids. They are also
rauvolfia serpentina.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

rheum officinale.dose: The individually correct dosage is the smallest dosage necessary to maintain a soft stool. The average dose is 0.5-1.5g of dried plant material or in decoction; preparations standardized to contain 10-30mg of hydroxyanthracene deriva- tives, usually taken at bedtime (21, 22, 28).
rheum officinale.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Short-term treatment of occasional constipation (20, 23, 24).
rheum officinale.formulations: Dried plant material and preparations standardized to contain 10-30mg of hydroxyanthracene derivatives per dose (21, 22). Package in well-closed, light- resistant containers (9, 11).
rheum officinale.precautions: As with other stimulant laxatives, products containing Rhizoma Rhei should not be administered to patients with intestinal obstruction and stenosis, atony, severe dehydration states with water and electrolyte depletion, or chronic constipation. Rhizoma Rhei should not be used in patients with inflammatory intestinal diseases, such as appendicitis, Crohn disease, ulcerative colitis, or irritable bo
rheum officinale.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

folium sennae.dose: The correct individual dose is the smallest required to produce a comfortable, soft-formed motion (21). Powder: 1-2g of leaf daily at bedtime (11). Adults and children over 10 years: standardized daily dose equivalent to 10-30mg sennosides (calculated as sennoside B) taken at night.
folium sennae.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Short-term use in occasional constipation (21-25).
folium sennae.formulations: Crude plant material, powder, oral infusion, and extracts (liquid or solid) stan- dardized for content of sennosides A and B (15, 21, 22). Package in well-closed containers protected from light and moisture (1-8).
folium sennae.precautions: As with other stimulant laxatives, the drug is contraindicated in persons with ileus, intestinal obstruction, and stenosis, atony, undiagnosed abdominal symp- toms, inflammatory colonopathies, appendicitis, abdominal pains of unknown
folium sennae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

cassia senna.dose: The correct individual dose is the smallest required to produce a comfortable, soft-formed motion (21). Powder, 1-2g of fruit daily at bedtime (8, 19, 20). Adults and children over 10 years: standardized daily dose equivalent to 10- 30mg sennosides (calculated as sennoside B) taken at night.
cassia senna.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Short-term use in occasional constipation (21-25).
cassia senna.formulations: Crude plant material, powder, oral infusion, and extracts (liquid or solid, standardized for content of sennosides A and B) (12, 20, 21). Package in well- closed containers protected from light and moisture (2-7).
cassia senna.precautions: As with other stimulant laxatives, the drug is contraindicated in cases of ileus, intestinal obstruction, stenosis, atony, undiagnosed abdominal symptoms, in- flammatory colonopathies, appendicitis, abdominal pains of unknown cause, severe dehydration states with water and electrolyte depletion, or chronic constipation (20, 21, 30). Fructus Sennae should not be used in children under the age of 10
cassia senna.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

thymus vulgaris.dose: Adults and children from 1 year: 1-2g of the dried herb or the equivalent amount of fresh herb as an oral infusion several times a day (30, 36); children up to 1 year: 0.5-1g (36). Fluid extract: dosage calculated according to the dosage of the herb (37). Tincture (1:10, 70% ethanol): 40 drops up to 3 times daily (38). Topical use: a 5% infusion as a gargle or mouth-wash (30, 38).
thymus vulgaris.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
thymus vulgaris.formulations: Dried herb for infusion, extract, and tincture (1).
thymus vulgaris.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

radix valerianae.dose: Dried root and rhizome, 2-3g drug per cup by oral infusion, 1-5 times per day, up to a total of 10g and preparations correspondingly (6, 22). Tincture (1:5, 70% ethanol), 0.5-1 teaspoon (1-3ml), once to several times a day. External use, 100g drug for a full bath (22).
radix valerianae.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data As a mild sedative and sleep-promoting agent (8, 12, 22-25). The drug is often
radix valerianae.formulations: Internal use as the expressed juice, tincture, extracts, and other galenical prepa- rations (8, 22). External use as a bath additive (22). Store in tightly closed containers, in a cool dry place, protected from light (1-6).
radix valerianae.precautions: Radix Valerianae should not be used during pregnancy or lactation (31, 34).
radix valerianae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

rhizoma zingiberis.dose: For motion sickness in adults and children more than 6 years: 0.5g, 2-4 times daily. Dyspepsia, 2-4g daily, as powdered plant material or extracts (21).
rhizoma zingiberis.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data The prophylaxis of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness (20- 23), postoperative nausea (24), pernicious vomiting in pregnancy (25), and seasickness (26, 27).
rhizoma zingiberis.formulations: Dried root powder, extract, tablets and tincture (2, 14). Powdered ginger should be stored in well-closed containers (not plastic) which prevent access of mois- ture. Store protected from light in a cool, dry place (4, 5).
rhizoma zingiberis.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-1

radix althaeae.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) For pyrexia: a decoction from 3g crude drug, twice daily (1, 5). For the common cold: 1.5-3.0g powdered crude drug three times daily, after meals and at bedtime (1). For diarrhoea: a decoction from 3-9g crude drug as a single dose as needed (1, 5), or two tablets of 500mg four times daily, after meals and at bedtime (5).
radix althaeae.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Prophylaxis and symptomatic treatment of upper respiratory infections, such as the common cold and uncomplicated sinusitis (17-19), bronchitis (6, 9) and pharyngotonsillitis (20), lower urinary tract infections (21) and acute diarrhoea (22, 23).
radix althaeae.formulations: Crude drug, capsules, tablets and pills (1, 2, 6). Store in a well-closed container, protected from light and moisture.
radix althaeae.precautions: were reported in either group (17). A randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind pilot trial was conducted
radix althaeae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

angelica sinensis diels.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 4.5-9g crude drug (1).
angelica sinensis diels.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None. Although Radix Angelicae Sinensis has been alleged to be useful for the treatment of menopausal symptoms, a randomized, placebo-controlled clinical trial concluded that 4.5g of the root daily for 24 weeks did not alleviate menopausal symptoms, such as hot flushes (14).
angelica sinensis diels.formulations: Powdered crude drug and fluidextracts (4). Store in an airtight container in a cool, dry place protected from moisture (1).
angelica sinensis diels.precautions: Radix Angelicae Sinensis should not be administered to children or patients with diarrhoea, haemorrhagic diseases or hypermenorrhoea, and should not be used during pregnancy or lactation (4).
angelica sinensis diels.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

pharmacokinetics, 1995, 20:55-60..dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Topical application: an infusion of 1-2g/150ml (18). External use: a 40% alcohol extract (1:1), or tincture (1:5) in 90% alcohol (2). For the treatment of wounds, the tincture is applied undiluted; for compresses, the tincture is usually diluted at least 1:3 with sterile water (18, 48, 49). Ointment: 2-5% (48, 50).
pharmacokinetics, 1995, 20:55-60..therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
pharmacokinetics, 1995, 20:55-60..formulations: Infusion for topical use; aqueous and alcohol extracts, tinctures and ointment for external use (2, 18, 19). Store in a well-closed container, protected from light (1).
pharmacokinetics, 1995, 20:55-60..precautions: Flos Calendulae is contraindicated in cases of known allergy to plants of the Asteraceae (Compositae) family (18).
pharmacokinetics, 1995, 20:55-60..source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

holkema, 1988:113-114..therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
holkema, 1988:113-114..formulations: Crude drug, extracts, tincture (1:5, 25% ethanol), lozenges and mouthwash. Store in a well-closed container, protected from light (1, 5).
holkema, 1988:113-114..precautions: Flos Caryophylli is contraindicated in cases of known allergy to plants of the Myrtaceae family.
holkema, 1988:113-114..source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

cimicifuga racemosa.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 40-60% isopropyl alcohol or ethanol extracts of the crude drug (18-20, 22-26, 37, 42), corresponding to 40mg drug (27).
cimicifuga racemosa.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Treatment of climacteric symptoms such as hot flushes, profuse sweating, sleeping disorders and nervous irritability (18-26).
cimicifuga racemosa.formulations: Crude drug, and isopropyl alcohol or ethanol extracts (16). Store in a well- closed container, protected from light and moisture.
cimicifuga racemosa.precautions: Owing to its potential estrogenic effects (39) and the lack of data on its safety,
cimicifuga racemosa.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

rhizoma cimicifugae racemosae.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 160-900mg dried 45% ethanol or 70% methanol extract (drug: extract ratio 4-7:1) standardized to contain 18.75% oligomeric procyanidins (calculated as epi-catechin) or 2.2% flavonoids (calculated as hyperoside), respectively (26-29, 31-34, 84); 1.0-1.5g comminuted crude drug as an infu- sion 3-4 times daily (35). Therapeutic effects may require 4-6 weeks of continuous therapy (84).
rhizoma cimicifugae racemosae.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Treatment of chronic congestive heart failure stage II, as defined by the New York Heart Association (24-34).
rhizoma cimicifugae racemosae.formulations: Crude drug for infusion and hydroalcoholic extracts (35). Store in a well-closed container, protected from light and moisture (1).
rhizoma cimicifugae racemosae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

naturheilverfahren, 1995..dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 2-3g powdered crude drug or equivalent preparations (20).
naturheilverfahren, 1995..therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data As a prophylactic and restorative tonic for enhancement of mental and physi- cal capacities in cases of weakness, exhaustion and tiredness, and during con- valescence (4, 18-20).
naturheilverfahren, 1995..formulations: Powdered crude drug or extracts in capsules, tablets, teas, syrups, fluidextracts (63). Store in a well-closed container, protected from light (3).
naturheilverfahren, 1995..precautions: Radix Eleutherococci should not be used during pregnancy or lactation, or by patients with blood pressure in excess of 180/90mmHg (24/12kPa) (4). Radix
naturheilverfahren, 1995..source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

eucalyptus globulus labill.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
eucalyptus globulus labill.formulations: Essential oil in solid, semisolid or liquid preparations (1) and galenical prepara- tions (17). Store in a well-filled, tightly closed container, protected from heat and light (1, 2).
eucalyptus globulus labill.precautions: Preparations of Aetheroleum Eucalypti should not be administered internally to children (34), or patients with inflammation of the gastrointestinal tract, gall bladder disease or impaired liver function (4, 17, 34). Aetheroleum Euca- lypti should not be taken internally during pregnancy (35), see Precautions.
eucalyptus globulus labill.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

the stem and.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Short-term treatment of occasional constipation (1, 9-11). As a single dose, for total intestinal evacuation before X-rays and other diagnostic examinations
the stem and.formulations: Finely cut and powdered crude drug, powder, dried extract, liquid and solid preparations (8). Store in a tightly closed, light-resistant container for a maximum of 3 years (1, 2).
the stem and.precautions: Cortex Frangulae should not be administered to patients with intestinal obstruction and stenosis, atony, inflammatory diseases of the colon (such as ulcerative colitis, irritable bowel syndrome, Crohn disease), appendicitis, severe dehydration with water and electrolyte depletion, or chronic constipation (9, 19, 23). As with other stimulant laxatives, Cortex Frangulae is contraindicated in patient
the stem and.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

hamamelis virginiana.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) External use: steam distillate, undiluted or diluted 1:3 with water to make poul- tices; 20-30% in semisolid preparations (3). Extracts: in semisolid and liquid preparations corresponding to 5-10% of the crude drug (3, 5). Crude drug: decoctions from 5-10g to 1 cup (250ml) water for poultices and wound irrigation (3, 5). Rectal suppositories: 1-3 times daily the quantity of a prepa- ration corresponding to 0.1-1.0g crude drug, use Hamamelidis water undiluted or diluted 1:3 with water (3, 5). Other preparations: several times daily, corre- sponding to 0.1-1.0g drug in preparations, or witch hazel water undiluted or diluted with water (3).
hamamelis virginiana.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Topically for minor skin lesions, bruises and sprains (3, 5, 20), local inflamma- tion of the skin and mucous membranes (3, 5, 20-24), haemorrhoids (3, 5, 20, 25-28) and varicose veins (3).
hamamelis virginiana.formulations: Dried leaves and bark for decoctions; steam distillate, ointment and supposi- tories (3, 9). Fresh leaves and twigs are collected in the spring and early summer to make a steam distillate (3). Store in a well-closed container, protected from light (19).
hamamelis virginiana.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

aesculus hippocastanum.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 250.0-312.5mg twice daily of a standardized powdered extract of the crude drug (equivalent to 100mg aescin) containing 16-20% triterpene glycosides, calculated as aescin (55); topical gels containing 2% aescin (22-24, 49).
aesculus hippocastanum.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Internally, for treatment of symptoms of chronic venous insufficiency, includ- ing pain, feeling of heaviness in the legs, nocturnal calf-muscle spasms, itching and oedema (13-21). Externally, for the symptomatic treatment of chronic venous insufficiency, sprains and bruises (22-24).
aesculus hippocastanum.formulations: Crude drug and extracts (7). Store away from light and humidity (1).
aesculus hippocastanum.precautions: Semen Hippocastani is contraindicated in cases of known allergy to plants of the Hippocastanaceae family.
aesculus hippocastanum.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

american botanical council, 1998..dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 2-4g crude drug (32). Internal use: standardized tinctures or fluidextracts (23, 98, 100), or standardized hydroethanolic or dried hydro- methanolic extracts, up to a daily dose of 900mg extract (equivalent to 0.2- 2.7mg total hypericin) (19, 21, 22, 27, 31).
american botanical council, 1998..formulations: Dried crude drug for decoction, powdered drug or extracts in capsules, tablets, tinctures and drops (2, 7, 32). Topical preparations include the oil, infusions, compresses, gels and ointments. Store in a well-closed container, protected from light (10, 11).
american botanical council, 1998..precautions: Herba Hyperici is contraindicated in cases of known allergy to plants of the Clusiaceae family.
american botanical council, 1998..source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

melaleuca alternifolia cheel.formulations: Essential oil (1, 2). Store in a well-filled, airtight container, protected from heat and light.
melaleuca alternifolia cheel.precautions: Aetheroleum Melaleucae Alternifoliae is contraindicated in cases of known allergy to plants of the Myrtaceae family.
melaleuca alternifolia cheel.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

melissa officinalis.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Externally, for symptomatic treatment of herpes labialis (16-18).
melissa officinalis.formulations: Comminuted crude drug; powder, tea bags, dried and fluidextracts for infusions and other galenical preparations (7, 14, 15). Store in a tightly closed container, protected from light (1). Do not store in plastic containers (7).
melissa officinalis.precautions: External use: none. Internal use: see Precautions.
melissa officinalis.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

folium melissae.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Internally for symptomatic treatment of irritable bowel syndrome (15-20), and digestive disorders such as flatulence and gastritis (21-23). Externally for treat- ment of myalgia and headache (21, 24-27).
folium melissae.formulations: Essential oil, concentrated peppermint emulsion, peppermint spirit and other galenic preparations (1, 21). Store in a well-closed container, protected from light (1, 2).
folium melissae.precautions: Preparations of Aetheroleum Menthae Piperitae should not be used internally by patients with inflammation of the gastrointestinal tract or gall bladder, or with impaired liver function (21). Hypersensitivity to the essential oil has been reported (55-57).
folium melissae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

piperitae consists.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 1-3g crude drug three times daily (14, 27). Infusion: pour 150ml hot water over 1.5-3.0g (one tablespoon) dried leaves, steep for 10 minutes, strain and drink three times daily between meals (6, 15, 28). Tincture: 2-3ml (1:5, 45% ethanol) three times daily (14).
piperitae consists.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
piperitae consists.formulations: Dried leaves (2, 3). Tincture and infusions (6). Store in a well-closed container, protected from light (2).
piperitae consists.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

oenothera biennis.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 320-480mg fixed oil (calculated as g-linolenic acid) in divided doses for atopic eczema, and 240-320mg in divided doses for mastalgia (19).
oenothera biennis.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Internally for symptomatic treatment of atopic eczema (2, 16-21), diabetic neuropathy (22, 23), and mastalgia (24-26). Clinical evidence for its use in the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis (27-30) is conflicting, as are the results of trials in women with premenstrual syndrome (31-35). Further well-designed clinical trials are needed to clarify these data. The results from clinical trials do not support the use of Oleum Oenotherae Biennis for the treatment of cli
oenothera biennis.formulations: Fixed oil, neat or in capsule form (1, 13). Store in a well-filled, airtight glass container, protected from heat and light.
oenothera biennis.precautions: were higher in patients treated with danazol and bromocrip- tine (30% and 35%, respectively) than in those treated with the fixed oil (4%) (24).
oenothera biennis.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

methystici consists.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: crude drug and extracts equivalent to 60-210mg kava pyrones (15, 18, 21-24).
methystici consists.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Short-term symptomatic treatment of mild states of anxiety or insomnia, due to nervousness, stress or tension (14-24).
methystici consists.formulations: Comminuted crude drug and extracts for oral use (15). Store in a tightly closed container, away from light.
methystici consists.precautions: During pregnancy and lactation, and in patients with endogenous depression (15) or liver disease.1
methystici consists.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

the trunk of.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 75-200mg lipidosterolic extract of the crude drug, in divided doses. To minimize gastrointestinal disturbances, take with food or milk.
the trunk of.formulations: Lipophilic extract of the crude drug (1, 2). Store in a cool, dry place.
the trunk of.precautions: Cortex Pruni Africanae is contraindicated in cases of known allergy to plants of the Rosaceae family. It is also contraindicated during pregnancy and lacta- tion and in children under the age of 12 years because of its effects on andro- gen and estrogen metabolism (39, 86).
the trunk of.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

rhamnus purshiana.dose: The correct dosage for the treatment of occasional constipation is the smallest dosage necessary to maintain a soft stool. Daily dosage: 0.3-1.0g crude drug in a single dose (20); all preparations standardized to contain 20-30mg of hydroxyanthracene derivatives calculated as cascaroside A; taken at bedtime, or in two divided doses, one in the morning and one at bedtime (20, 21).
rhamnus purshiana.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Short-term treatment of occasional constipation (8, 17, 20, 21).
rhamnus purshiana.formulations: Finely cut crude drug, powder, dried extracts, extract (5), fluidextract (5), other liquid and solid preparations (5, 7). Store in a tightly sealed, light-resistant container (1, 3).
rhamnus purshiana.precautions: Cortex Rhamni Purshianae should not be administered to patients with intesti- nal obstruction and stenosis, atony, inflammatory diseases of the colon (such as ulcerative colitis, irritable bowel syndrome, Crohn disease), appendicitis,
rhamnus purshiana.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

sambuci consists.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: crude drug 3-5g as an infusion (preferably taken hot) three times daily; 25% ethanol extract 3-5ml; tincture (1:5 in 25% ethanol) 10-25ml (22).
sambuci consists.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
sambuci consists.formulations: Crude drug for decoctions and infusions (crude drug also available in tea bags); as a component of multi-ingredient products (7). Store in a well-closed con- tainer, protected from light (2).
sambuci consists.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

polygala senega.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 1.5-3.0g crude drug as an infusion or decoction in divided doses (18, 35). A 60% ethanol extract (made slightly alkaline with dilute ammonia): 0.9-3ml; tincture: 2.5-7.5g. Equivalent preparations (18).
polygala senega.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
polygala senega.formulations: Chopped crude drug for decoctions and extracts (6, 18). Store in a tightly closed container, protected from light and humidity (3).
polygala senega.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

serenoa repens.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 1-2g crude drug or 320mg (as a single dose or 160mg twice daily) of a lipidosterolic extract (n-hexane, 90% ethanol or supercritical
serenoa repens.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Treatment of lower urinary tract symptoms (nocturia, polyuria, urinary reten- tion) secondary to BPH stages I and II, as defined by Alken (1, 4, 14-33), in cases where diagnosis of prostate cancer is negative.
serenoa repens.formulations: Crude drug, lipidosterolic extracts (n-hexane, 90% ethanol or fluid [carbon dioxide] supercritical extracts standardized to contain 70-95% free fatty acids and corresponding ethyl esters), and preparations thereof. Store in a tightly closed container in a cool, dry place.
serenoa repens.precautions: Owing to its effects on androgen and estrogen metabolism, the use of Fructus
serenoa repens.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

silybum marianum.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 12-15g crude drug (35); 200-400mg silymarin, calculated as silybin, in standardized preparations (35). A parenteral preparation, silybin hemisuccinate sodium salt, is available
silybum marianum.formulations: Usually standardized extracts for phytomedicine; crude drug for decoction (4). Store in a well-closed container, protected from light and humidity (2).
silybum marianum.precautions: Fructus Silybi Mariae is contraindicated in cases of known allergy to plants of the Asteraceae family (120).
silybum marianum.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

tanacetum parthenium schultz.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: encapsulated crude drug equivalent to 0.2-0.6mg parthenolide (as a chemical marker) for prevention of migraine (21-23, 27, 32).
tanacetum parthenium schultz.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Prevention of migraine (20-24). Although Herba Tanaceti Parthenii has been used for treatment of rheumatoid arthritis, a clinical study failed to prove any beneficial effects (25).
tanacetum parthenium schultz.formulations: Crude drug for decoction; powdered drug or extracts in capsules, tablets, tinc- tures and drops (2). Store in a well-closed container, protected from light and humidity (3).
tanacetum parthenium schultz.precautions: Herba Tanaceti Parthenii is contraindicated in cases of known allergy to plants of the Asteraceae family, and during pregnancy (1, 19, 28).
tanacetum parthenium schultz.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

urtica dioica.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage: 4-6g crude drug or equivalent preparations as an infusion (4, 56); 600-1200mg dried 20% methanol extract (5:1) (22, 25, 27, 31, 32); 1.5-7.5ml 45% ethanol extract (1:1) (29); 5ml 40% ethanol extract (1:5) (17, 23).
urtica dioica.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Symptomatic treatment of lower urinary tract disorders (nocturia, polyuria, urinary retention) resulting from BPH stages I and II, as defined by Alken, in cases where diagnosis of prostate cancer is negative (22-35).
urtica dioica.formulations: Crude drug for infusion; hydroalcoholic extracts (4, 56). Store in a well-closed container, protected from light and humidity (2, 13).
urtica dioica.precautions: Radix Urticae is contraindicated in cases of known allergy to plants of the Urticaceae family. Owing to its effects on androgen and estrogen metabolism,
urtica dioica.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

arctostaphylos uva.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dose: 3g crude drug in 150ml water as an infusion or cold macerate, up to three or four times daily; 400-850mg hydroquinone derivatives. Other preparations accordingly calculated as arbutin (12, 13). Patients should avoid highly acidic foods, such as acidic fruits or fruit juice, during treatment (25, 40), and be advised to drink plenty of fluids.
arctostaphylos uva.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
arctostaphylos uva.formulations: Crude drug for infusions or cold macerates, extracts and solid forms for oral administration (13). Store in a well-closed container, protected from light (3).
arctostaphylos uva.precautions: During pregnancy (33) or lactation, or in children under the age of 12 years (13). Folium Uvae Ursi is also contraindicated in patients with kidney disorders (12).
arctostaphylos uva.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-2

vitex agnus.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Dry native extract: 8.3-12.5:1 (w/w), approximately 1.0% casticin: 1 tablet containing 2.6-4.2 mg native extract, swallowed whole with some liquid each morning (4). Dry native extract: 9.58-11.5:1 (w/w): 1 tablet containing 3.5-4.2 mg na- tive extract each morning with some liquid (22). Dry native extract: 6.0-12.0:1 (w/w), approximately 0.6% casticin. For premenstrual syndrome: 1 tablet containing 20 mg native extract daily with water (63). Fluidextract: 1:1 (g/ml), 70% alcohol (v/v): 0.5-1.0 ml (9). Tincture: ethanol 58% (100 g of aqueous-alcoholic solution contains 9 g of 1:5 tincture): 40 drops, once daily with some liquid each morning (4). 26
vitex agnus.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Orally for the symptomatic treatment of gynaecological disorders includ- ing corpus luteum insufficiency and hyperprolactinaemia (19), premen- strual syndrome (20-25), menstrual irregularities (26, 27), cyclic mastalgia (28, 29) and also to treat hormonally-induced acne (30, 31). 13
vitex agnus.formulations: Crude drug, extracts, fluidextracts, tinctures and infusions. The dried ber- ries should be stored in airtight non-plastic containers and protected from light, heat, moisture and insect infestation (4).
vitex agnus.precautions: Adverse reactions have been reported in some clinical trials. A review of 30 studies involving 11 506 subjects reported a total of 246 adverse events, thus representing an adverse reaction rate of approximately 2% (4). The major reactions reported included acne, changes to the menstrual cycle, dizziness, gastrointestinal distress, increased menstrual flow, nausea, skin reactions, urticaria and wei
vitex agnus.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

berberis vulgaris.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dose: crude drug 0.5-1.0 g three times daily, or by decoction; liquid extract 1:1 in 60% ethanol, 0.3-1.0 ml three times daily; tincture 1:10 60% ethanol, 2-4 ml three times daily (1).
berberis vulgaris.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
berberis vulgaris.formulations: Crude drug and dried extracts, fluidextracts, and tinctures (1, 75). Store in a tightly sealed container away from heat and light.
berberis vulgaris.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

gummi boswellii.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Crude drug: 1-3 g daily (frequency not specified) (1). Extracts: 300-350 mg three times daily (19-21). 58
gummi boswellii.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Orally for the management of arthritis, bronchial asthma, Crohn’s disease and ulcerative colitis (18-22).
gummi boswellii.precautions: Minor gastrointestinal side-effects have been reported in the clinical trials (19-22).
gummi boswellii.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

elettaria cardamo.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Average daily dosage: 1.5 g of drug; equivalent preparations. Tincture: daily dosage equivalent to 12 g (23).
elettaria cardamo.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None. Uses described in pharmacopoeias and well-established documents Orally for the treatment of dyspepsia (23, 24). O CH3 CH3 CH3 1,8-Cineole CH3 O H3C CH3 H3C O H CH3 HO H3C CH3 H
elettaria cardamo.formulations: and storage Crude drug, extracts and tinctures (23).
elettaria cardamo.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

cucurbita pepo.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Oral daily dose: 10 g of seed; equivalent preparations (16).
cucurbita pepo.precautions: Gastrointestinal disorders (heartburn, nausea, stomach ache and diar- rhoea), allergic skin reactions and tinnitus.
cucurbita pepo.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

cynara cardunculus.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Average oral daily dose: for hypercholesterolaemia and dyspepsia, 1-2 g of a dried aqueous extract (24, 27, 45). Adult daily dose: 5-10 g of crude drug; or equivalent preparations (15, 17).
cynara cardunculus.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Treatment of digestive complaints (e.g. dyspepsia, feeling of fullness, flat- ulence, nausea, stomach ache and vomiting) (15, 22, 23). Adjunct treat- ment of mild to moderate hypercholesterolaemia (22, 24-27). HO HO
cynara cardunculus.formulations: Crude drug, extracts and other Galenical preparations for internal use.
cynara cardunculus.precautions: Gastrointestinal complaints included mild diarrhoea, accompanied by ab- dominal cramps, upper abdominal pain, nausea and heartburn. Allergic reactions may occur in sensitized patients (22, 25).
cynara cardunculus.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

punica grana.dose: Oral daily dose: 3-9 g for decoction (19). Daily dose: 20 g root bark fluidextract (1:1) in 59% ethanol for the treat- ment of Taenia infestation (27).
punica grana.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data No information was found. N H3C O
punica grana.formulations: Crude drug, extracts and tablets. Store in a cool dry place (19). 115
punica grana.precautions: Common adverse events observed in humans include dizziness, visual disturbances, weakness, calf spasms and tremors. Large overdoses (> 80.0 g) of the crude drug may lead to dizziness, mydriasis, severe head- ache, vertigo, vomiting, lethargy, collapse and possible death due to the alkaloid content (26).
punica grana.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

pericarpium granati.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Oral daily dose: 2.5-4.5 g (1, 7).
pericarpium granati.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
pericarpium granati.formulations: Crude drug, extracts and tablets. Store in a cool dry place (1).
pericarpium granati.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

psidium gua.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) As a mouthwash: 15 ml of aqueous extract three times daily for at least 1 minute per session (26). For diarrhoea: 500 mg of the powdered leaf three or four times daily (50).
psidium gua.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Oral treatment of acute diarrhoea, gingivitis and rotaviral enteritis (25-28).
psidium gua.formulations: Crude drug, decoctions, extracts and teas.
psidium gua.precautions: One report of allergic dermatitis has been recorded after external applica- tion of a tea prepared from the crude drug (51).
psidium gua.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

lichen islandicus.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) (3, 17, 18) As a demulcent to treat inflammation and dryness of the pharyngeal mu- cosa: comminuted herb for infusions and other Galenical formulations for internal use. For loss of appetite: comminuted herb, cold macerates for internal use. Average daily dosage: 4-6 g of herb in divided doses, or equivalent preparations (17). Infusion: 1.5 g of crude drug in 150 ml water. Fluid- extract 1:1 (g/ml): 4-6 ml. Tincture 1:5 (g/ml): 20-30 ml (18).
lichen islandicus.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Used orally as a demulcent to treat inflammation and dryness of the pha- ryngeal mucosa (15, 16).
lichen islandicus.formulations: Crude drug, extracts, fluidextract, infusion and tinctures (3, 17).
lichen islandicus.precautions: Allergic reactions, abdominal pain and nausea have been reported. Few adverse events were observed in a study of 3143 children aged 4-12 years who were administered 320-600 mg of an aqueous extract of the thalli in lozenges per day over a 1-2 week period. The adverse events were mild and transient (30).
lichen islandicus.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

fructus macrocarponii.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) For the prevention of UTIs in adults the recommended daily dose of cran- berry juice is 30-300 ml of a 30% pure juice product; for the treatment of UTIs in adults the daily dosage range is 360-960 ml or equivalent (1). Capsules containing a concentrated cranberry extract: 1-6 capsules daily, equivalent to 3 fluid ounces (90 ml) cranberry juice or 400-450 mg cranberry solids (1).
fructus macrocarponii.formulations: Crude drug, extracts, juice, tablets, capsules. Store in a well-closed con- tainer, in a refrigerator (2).
fructus macrocarponii.precautions: One rare case of immune-related thrombocytopaenia has been reported in a 68-year-old patient (45). Nausea and severe vomiting were reported in a 4-month-old infant after administration of 180 ml of cranberry juice (46).
fructus macrocarponii.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

cortex magnoliae.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) For oral administration: 3-9 g of crude drug for decoction daily in divided doses (2, 19).
cortex magnoliae.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data No information was found.
cortex magnoliae.formulations: Crude drug, decoction. Store in a tightly sealed container away from heat and light (2).
cortex magnoliae.precautions: Hypersensitivity or allergy to the plant material.
cortex magnoliae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

herba millefolii.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) (12) Internal: 4.5 g of cut herb (flowering top) per day, or 3.0 g cut flowers for teas (infusions) and other Galenical preparations; pressed juice of freshly harvested herb. Infusion: 1-2 g in 150 ml boiled water for 10-15 minutes, three times dai- ly between meals. Succus (pressed juice from fresh herb): 5 ml (1 teaspoon), three times dai- ly between meals. Fluidextract 1:1 (g/ml): 1-2 ml, three times daily between meals. Tincture (1:5 g/ml): 5 ml, three times daily between meals. External: sitz bath: 100 g per 20 litres of warm or hot water (12).
herba millefolii.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
herba millefolii.formulations: Crude drug, extracts, fluidextract, infusions, succus (pressed juice from fresh herb) and tinctures.
herba millefolii.precautions: Numerous reports of allergic contact dermatitis have been published (33- 39). In clinical testing, product formulations containing 2% of extracts of the crude drug were generally not irritating. In provocative testing, pa- tients reacted to a Compositae mix that contained the crude drug, as well as to the crude drug alone. In clinical testing, a formulation containing 0.1% yarrow extract (propylen
herba millefolii.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

fructus momordicae.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Adult daily oral dose: 10-15 ml of fresh juice (1); 2-15 g of dried crude drug (25, 28, 30).
fructus momordicae.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
fructus momordicae.formulations: Crude drug, extracts, fruit juice and tablets.
fructus momordicae.precautions: The reported adverse effects of the crude drug include hypoglycaemic coma and convulsions in children, increases in G-glutamyl transferase and alkaline phosphatase levels, and headaches (53).
fructus momordicae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

vaccinium myrtillus.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Internal: daily dosage of crude drug 20-60 g (2). Extracts: 80-160 mg of extract standardized to 25% anthocyanosides (three times daily). The dose of anthocyanosides is 20-40 mg three times daily (20). External: 10% decoction; equivalent preparations (20). 223
vaccinium myrtillus.formulations: Crude drug, extracts, tablets and capsules.
vaccinium myrtillus.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

panax quinque.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Oral dose: 3-9 g daily in divided doses (2).
panax quinque.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
panax quinque.formulations: Crude drug, extracts, tinctures and capsules.
panax quinque.precautions: No adverse reactions have been reported.
panax quinque.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

phellodendron amu.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dose: 3-10 g daily (1).
phellodendron amu.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
phellodendron amu.formulations: Crude drug and dried extracts, fluidextracts and tinctures. Store in a tight- ly sealed container away from heat and light (3). 254
phellodendron amu.precautions: Berberine is well tolerated in humans in therapeutic daily doses of 0.5 g (61).
phellodendron amu.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

neopicrorhiza scrophulariiflora hong.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Oral daily dose: 1-3 g of the powdered crude drug (1).
neopicrorhiza scrophulariiflora hong.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None. Uses described in pharmacopoeias and well-established documents Used orally to treat fever, immune disorders and skin diseases (1, 2). While two studies have suggested a possible role of the rhizome for the treat- ment of bronchial asthma (17, 18) and viral hepatitis (19), no randomized controlled clinical trials have been performed. 262
neopicrorhiza scrophulariiflora hong.formulations: Crude drug, extracts, tablets and tinctures.
neopicrorhiza scrophulariiflora hong.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

aetheroleum rosmarini.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Daily dosage for oral administration: 1 ml of essential oil (24). External use: 6-10% essential oil in semi-solid and liquid preparations (24).
aetheroleum rosmarini.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
aetheroleum rosmarini.formulations: Essential oil for oral and external use (24) and aromatherapy (13, 22). Store in a cool place in an airtight container, protected from light (28).
aetheroleum rosmarini.precautions: Following oral use gastrointestinal complaints and hypersensitivity reac- tions may occur rarely. Inhalation can occasionally cause irritation and very rarely laryngospasm (24). External use may worsen bronchospasm. Rarely hypersensitivity reac- tions of the skin may occur. Photoaggravated allergic contact dermatitis (25, 26) and cheilitis (27) have been reported. 291
aetheroleum rosmarini.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

rosmarinus offi.dose: (Unless otherwise specified) Daily dosage: for oral use 4-6 g of herb. Infusion: 2-4 g in 150 ml water three times daily. Fluidextract (1:1, 45% ethanol w/w) 1.5-3.0 ml daily. Tincture (1:5, 70% ethanol) 3-8.5 ml daily. Dry extract (4.5-5.5:1 w/w) 0.36-0.44 g, three times daily. External use: boil 50 g of herb in 1 l of wa- ter, add to one full bath (47).
rosmarinus offi.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
rosmarinus offi.formulations: Crude drug for infusions, dry extracts, fluidextract and other Galenical preparations for internal and external use (47).
rosmarinus offi.precautions: There is a single case-report of photoaggravated allergic contact dermati- tis, in which a patient developed contact dermatitis after handling the leaves of the plant on a sunny day (42-44). One case of cheilitis has been reported (45). A 56-year-old man developed occupational contact derma- titis of his hands, forearms, and face after coming into contact with an extract of the leaves. He reacted
rosmarinus offi.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

salix alba.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Adult oral daily dose: extracts, tinctures or fluidextracts equivalent to 120-240 mg of total salicin, or 6-12 g of powdered drug material as a de- coction (corresponding to 120-240 mg of total salicin) in two divided doses (1, 16, 33).
salix alba.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data Used orally for the symptomatic treatment of fever and pain, and symp- tomatic treatment of mild rheumatic conditions (14-20).
salix alba.formulations: Crude drug, dried hydroalcoholic or aqueous extracts, tinctures and fluidextracts (1, 16, 33).
salix alba.precautions: Allergic reactions such as pruritus, urticaria, asthma and gastrointestinal symptoms may occur (16). One case of an allergic reaction in a 32-year-old
salix alba.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

flos trifolii.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Oral dose: extracts of crude drug: 240-480 mg corresponding to 40- 80 mg/day of isoflavones (15, 17, 28, 29).
flos trifolii.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
flos trifolii.precautions: Ingestion of large amounts of clover in animal feed has been associated with a number of adverse effects in sheep in Australia. A publication on “clover disease” described symptoms of infertility, abnormal lactation, dystocia and prolapsed uterus, all of which were hypothetically attributed to the estrogenic effects of isoflavones (42). None of the controlled, clini- cal trials has reported advers
flos trifolii.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

ramulus cum uncis uncariae.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Oral daily dose: 3-12 g of the crude drug, added to a decoction, taken in divided doses (1).
ramulus cum uncis uncariae.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data None.
ramulus cum uncis uncariae.formulations: Crude drug (1). Store in a dry place (1).
ramulus cum uncis uncariae.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

viburnum prunifolium.dose: (Unless otherwise indicated) Powdered bark: 2.5-5 g as an infusion or decoction three times daily. Liq- uid extract (1:1) in 70% ethanol: dose 4-8 ml three times daily. Tincture (1:5 in 70% ethanol): 5-10 ml three times daily (1, 3).
viburnum prunifolium.therapeutic_uses: Uses supported by clinical data No information was found. 369
viburnum prunifolium.formulations: Crude drug, aqueous and ethanol extracts, fluidextracts and tinctures. Stor- age: protect from air, light, moisture, excessive heat and insect infestation (3).
viburnum prunifolium.source: WHO Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, WHO-vol-4

centalla asiatica.source: Ayurvedic Pharmacopoeia of India, Part I, API-Vol-4
